name: the primitive type called ado

VINIT.justification: the state before a supervisory relationship is established

TANCH.justification: the boundary of the hierarchy that contains all supervisory links

AFWD.justification: the directed flow of authority from supervisor to subordinate

AREV.justification: the return of information from subordinate to supervisor

CLINK.justification: the sequential composition of multiple supervisory levels

IMSCRIB.justification: the internal identification of an entity as a supervisor or subordinate

FSPLIT.justification: a single command splitting into a task execution path and a monitoring path

FFUSE.justification: the reunification of execution results and monitoring data into a single report

EVALT.justification: the affirmative state where the subordinate executes the command

EVALF.justification: the negative state where the command is rejected or fails

ENGAGR.justification: a state where the subordinate is simultaneously acting and deciding independently

IFIX.justification: the permanent record of the supervisory transaction

phase_3.both_description: active delegation where authority is shared and contested

steps.domain_action: branch command into execution and monitoring streams
confirm subordinate compliance in execution stream
reconstitute command status from execution and monitoring
compose status into a higher level report
record the supervisory event in the audit log

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the reduction in local entropy through order is balanced by signaling dissipation
